facts.text: Barrington Historical Society, founded in 1989, documents and interprets the town’s history while offering educational and recreational opportunities for residents.
The volunteer‑driven society preserves and promotes Barrington’s rich heritage, serving as a valuable community resource.
It operates the Joseph F. museum and hosts walking tours, lectures, workshops, and annual events like the Harvest Fest and Arts & Crafts Fair.
Barrington is officially a borough—a municipal form common in New Jersey, with 253 boroughs statewide as of 2023.
The Borough Act of 1987 streamlined local law, clearing accumulated amendments and contradictions that had built up over a century.
Barrington’s Historical Society is located on South 18th Street, a central spot for community history and events.
